title: Alternative Investments Portfolio Diversification - Client 039
content: Alternative investments encompass non-traditional asset classes offering return enhancement and correlation benefits beyond stocks and bonds. Alternative Categories: Private Equity provides access to non-public companies with potential for outsized returns. Private Credit offers attractive yields with floating rate structures. Real Assets include infrastructure, farmland, and timberland offering inflation protection. Hedge Fund Strategies employ long-short equity, arbitrage, and macro approaches targeting absolute returns. Access and Implementation: Client-039's $6.5M net worth qualifies as accredited investor enabling access to private opportunities. Minimum investment requirements typically $100K-$500K necessitate concentrated positions. Interval funds and publicly-traded alternatives provide lower minimums with monthly liquidity. Private Equity Allocation: 15% portfolio allocation ($975K) across three vintage years reducing timing risk. Focus on middle-market buyout funds with established track records. Target IRR: 14-18% over 7-10 year holding periods. J-curve dynamics require patient capital as early distributions lag capital calls. Private Credit: 10% allocation ($650K) through business development companies (BDCs) and direct lending funds. Yields of 9-12% with senior secured positions. Quarterly distributions supplement income needs. Lower correlation to public bond markets. Real Assets: 8% allocation ($520K) in infrastructure (toll roads, utilities, renewable energy) and farmland. Inflation-linked cash flows, essential service characteristics, trophy farmland appreciation. Expected returns: 8-10% with hedging properties. Hedge Funds: 7% allocation ($455K) through fund-of-funds providing strategy diversification. Long-short equity and merger arbitrage reduce market beta. Target 6-8% returns with dampened volatility. Liquidity Considerations: Stagger capital commitments, maintain public portfolio liquidity, 2-3 year liquid reserves. 60% total portfolio remains liquid.
